text.title: Implementing the Eviction Middleware
text.summary: Wire eviction as a tool-runtime decorator that token-counts every result, persists oversized ones, and exposes a read_file tool so the agent can recover slices.

section.title: Middleware shape
section.p: The eviction policy is a thin layer between tool dispatch and message append. It needs three pieces: a token counter, a persistent scratch path, and a recovery tool. None of the three involve the model.

rule.statement: Implement eviction as a runtime decorator with three components: (1) tokeniser-based size check on every tool return value, (2) deterministic write to a scratch directory keyed by tool-call-id, (3) a paired `read_file(path, lines)` tool the agent can call to recover slices on demand.
rule.rationale: Splitting these concerns mirrors LangChain Deep Agents' `filesystemMiddleware` and Manus's context-engineering write-up: the decision (evict or not) is deterministic, the storage is recoverable, and the agent only ever sees pointers — so the same architecture works whether the storage backend is local disk, S3, or an in-process content store.

example.code: # Pseudocode of the wrapper applied to every tool def run_tool_with_eviction(tool, args, *, threshold=20_000, scratch="/tmp/agent"): raw = tool(**args) n = count_tokens(raw) if n <= threshold: return raw # small — pass through path = f"{scratch}/{tool.__name__}_{uuid4().hex}.txt" Path(path).write_text(raw) return { "path": path, "preview": raw[:400], "total_tokens": n, "evicted": True, }
example.note: Threshold and scratch directory are config — knob lives in the runtime, not in the prompt.

example.code: # Eviction performed by the agent inside its own reasoning system_prompt = "If a tool result is huge, save it and remember the path." # The model still receives the full payload first, then decides.
example.note: Defeats the purpose — context damage occurs before the LLM can act.

antipattern.description: Hard-coding the threshold equal to the model's max context window.
antipattern.reason: Effective context (where accuracy holds) is far smaller than advertised — see the MECW paper. Set the threshold to a fraction of the working budget per step (e.g. 10–20% of the per-turn budget), not the model's nominal limit.

section.reference: Minimal Python middleware showing the wrapper, scratch layout, and recovery tool.
Manus — Context Engineering for AI Agents (filesystem-as-memory in production).
"Context Is What You Need" — empirical Maximum Effective Context Window measurements.
